objective: Authorize Argo execution of the Clotho Phase 1 implementation plan v9 (content address sha256:47f634889001eeeecc65390bc7e58e64d39d86f0c0061e0ff04afafacc556f8a; released by The Eye via PR #92, merge commit 73baad0caf5c3bee63dbdfc0672a7831644a12c1). The plan builds clotho/, an advisory zero-dependency knowledge-graph package proven by weaving TELOS itself. Approve ONLY if the plan is implementation-ready and consistent with its governing spec (v2.6), the repository's trust model (fail-closed, closed sets, spine read-only, advisory-only Clotho), and its own decisions and exit criteria.

blockers: codex has required edits: Route a specification amendment to The Eye and update the plan’s normative inventory table so `clotho-ledger-weaver` also requires `contract-files`, defined as the configured contract Markdown files opened, read, section-split, and uniquely indexed for clause-reference resolution. Require the ledger weaver to consume that driver-counted iterator even when `clotho-doc-weaver` is skipped, and extend manifest, completeness, skipped-weaver, and flagship tests accordingly.; Replace every illustrative D30 allowlist reference with one exhaustive normative mapping of loader-capable builtin specifiers to permitted named exports. At minimum, freeze the policy for both `module` and `node:module`, update `inventory.mjs` and tests to assert exact equality with that mapping, and reject every import, require, dynamic-import, re-export, namespace/default, or property-acquisition form not explicitly permitted.

codex has hard stops: The ledger weaver must resolve obligation references against current contract-section bytes, including when the documentation weaver is skipped, but its closed counted-source schema contains only `ledger-sources` and `run-sources`, and `ctx` provides no prevalidated contract map. Execution would therefore require an unaccounted contract-file read, consumption attributed to a skipped weaver, or omission of a required contract edge.; D30 repeatedly calls its loader-capable-builtin safe-export allowlist frozen, yet the plan supplies only examples (`builtinModules`, `isBuiltin`) rather than an exhaustive normative table. This leaves a security-sensitive closed set for the implementer to choose during execution.

authorization.note: Fail-closed: see gate.blockers and seat decisions.
